workflowEn.label: Name the sources
workflowEn.detail: Set the date, working hours, and sources ChatGPT may review.

workflowEn.label: Build today's brief
workflowEn.detail: Ask for priorities, meeting prep, replies needed, and decisions still open.

workflowEn.label: Check the evidence
workflowEn.detail: Open source links and separate confirmed facts from assumptions or access gaps.

workflowEn.label: Refine and repeat
workflowEn.detail: Mark what was useful or noisy, then schedule the brief only after it is reliable.

checkEn: Review source links and any inferred items before using the brief to plan or assign work.

workflowEn.label: Set a hypothesis
workflowEn.detail: Define a falsifiable question, a working baseline, and a measurable benchmark.

workflowEn.label: Build a small prototype
workflowEn.detail: Ask Codex for the smallest change, then run unit tests and microbenchmarks before training.

workflowEn.label: Run experiment loops
workflowEn.detail: Record metrics, runtime, memory, and failure modes after every benchmark run.

workflowEn.label: Review the evidence
workflowEn.detail: Decide whether to keep, revert, or refine the idea without claiming more than the results show.

checkEn: Have a domain expert review the method, matched comparisons, and replication before making general claims.

workflowEn.label: Gather feedback
workflowEn.detail: Name the Slack, GitHub, survey, or document sources ChatGPT should review.

workflowEn.label: Cluster the themes
workflowEn.detail: Create a Sheet or Doc with repeated themes, evidence, confidence, and open questions.

workflowEn.label: Review as a team
workflowEn.detail: Check the interpretation, split mixed themes, and select items for product or engineering.

workflowEn.label: Create follow-ups
workflowEn.detail: Draft updates or issues in the same chat and revisit them when new feedback arrives.

checkEn: Remove names and private quotes, and make sure every important theme links back to its source.

workflowEn.label: Attach the original
workflowEn.detail: Attach or reference the CSV or spreadsheet that needs cleaning.

workflowEn.label: Describe the issues
workflowEn.detail: Call out date formats, currencies, missing values, duplicates, or mixed-in summary rows.

workflowEn.label: Create a clean copy
workflowEn.detail: Request a new cleaned file and data-quality note without changing the original.

workflowEn.label: Compare the result
workflowEn.detail: Sample rows that changed, were removed, or could not be cleaned with confidence.

checkEn: Compare totals, row counts, source IDs, and blank values with the original before using the cleaned file.

workflowEn.label: Attach the data
workflowEn.detail: Attach the raw file or point to the Google Sheet for this question.

workflowEn.label: Ask one question
workflowEn.detail: Start with one clear business question and verify column names before calculating.

workflowEn.label: Create a visual answer
workflowEn.detail: Ask ChatGPT to calculate, summarize, and build a clear HTML visualization.

workflowEn.label: Drill down
workflowEn.detail: Open the chart and compare another period, group, or condition in the same chat.

checkEn: Verify metric definitions, filters, date ranges, and record counts before using the findings to decide.

workflowEn.label: Define the candidates
workflowEn.detail: Name the disease, drug targets to compare, and evidence sets required.

workflowEn.label: Search in parallel
workflowEn.detail: Review genetics, cohorts, clinical data, literature, and expression as separate workstreams.

workflowEn.label: Score consistently
workflowEn.detail: Apply one rubric to every evidence lane and separate direct evidence from related diseases.

workflowEn.label: Build the ranking
workflowEn.detail: Deliver a heatmap, final ranking, confidence, limitations, and reviewable raw payload.

checkEn: A scientist should review scoring, sources, and correlation-versus-causation before any decision.

workflowEn.label: Identify the dataset
workflowEn.detail: Provide the matrix, barcodes, features, manifest, and experiment metadata.

workflowEn.label: Set data-led thresholds
workflowEn.detail: Inspect distributions and propose justified QC thresholds instead of fixed defaults.

workflowEn.label: Inspect plots and counts
workflowEn.detail: Review threshold plots, the QC summary, and UMAP for removed cells or suspicious clusters.

workflowEn.label: Refine and rerun
workflowEn.detail: Adjust thresholds or add a reference atlas while preserving raw counts and prior reasoning.

checkEn: A bioinformatician should review thresholds, batch effects, and annotation confidence before biological use.

workflowEn.label: Prepare the inputs
workflowEn.detail: Provide a matched sample sheet, FASTQ files, transcriptome, genome, and GTF.

workflowEn.label: Check consistency
workflowEn.detail: Verify strandedness, reference consistency, and tool readiness before running.

workflowEn.label: Review QC
workflowEn.detail: Use MultiQC to inspect mapping rate, duplication, library type, and outlier samples.

workflowEn.label: Confirm the handoff
workflowEn.detail: Check the gene-level matrix and provenance before differential expression analysis.

checkEn: A specialist should verify library quality, sample metadata, and reference versions before analysis.

workflowEn.label: Gather cash inputs
workflowEn.detail: Add opening cash, receipts, payroll, payables, debt, tax, capex, and timing assumptions.

workflowEn.label: Build an editable model
workflowEn.detail: Use formulas and preserve the original weekly or monthly reporting cadence.

workflowEn.label: Find risk points
workflowEn.detail: Identify the cash low point, threshold breaches, and missing assumptions.

workflowEn.label: Test scenarios
workflowEn.detail: Change one driver at a time, such as collections or payment timing, to see its impact.

checkEn: Finance should tie balances to source data and approve assumptions before liquidity planning.

workflowEn.label: Attach baseline data
workflowEn.detail: Provide historical financials, valuation assumptions, and clearly sourced modeling notes.

workflowEn.label: Build the model
workflowEn.detail: Calculate operating drivers, unlevered free cash flow, WACC, terminal value, and enterprise value.

workflowEn.label: Audit the links
workflowEn.detail: Review source tie-outs, formulas, hardcodes, assumptions, and the sensitivity table.

workflowEn.label: Change one driver
workflowEn.detail: Vary growth, margin, capex, or working capital one assumption at a time.

checkEn: A finance expert should review formulas, WACC sources, and terminal value before investment use.

workflowEn.label: Attach plan and actuals
workflowEn.detail: Provide the budget, actuals export, and close notes from the same reporting period.

workflowEn.label: Map the accounts
workflowEn.detail: Map entries to budget categories and flag unclear items instead of forcing a match.

workflowEn.label: Calculate variances
workflowEn.detail: Show amount and percentage variances, with favorable or unfavorable logic by account type.

workflowEn.label: Review and explain
workflowEn.detail: Tie out the numbers and evidence before drafting the leadership summary.

checkEn: Finance should review category mapping, tie-outs, and any variance explanation without evidence.

workflowEn.label: Inspect the reference
workflowEn.detail: Review the deck, PDF, or images for aspect ratio, color, fonts, and layout patterns.

workflowEn.label: Assign slide roles
workflowEn.detail: Mark slides to preserve, rewrite, illustrate, or create, with clear layout rules.

workflowEn.label: Keep it editable
workflowEn.detail: Use live text, native charts for simple data, and visuals with one consistent direction.

workflowEn.label: Render and inspect
workflowEn.detail: Render every slide, check overflow, font substitution, and spacing, then fix issues before delivery.

checkEn: Verify the story, numbers, image rights, and rendered output of every slide on a real screen.

workflowEn.label: Set the inbox scope
workflowEn.detail: Choose the date range, labels, or message types the Gmail plugin may review.

workflowEn.label: Show your tone
workflowEn.detail: Provide sent emails or sample documents so drafts match your style.

workflowEn.label: Build a draft queue
workflowEn.detail: Separate messages to answer, defer, or enrich with context from Slack or documents.

workflowEn.label: Refine and schedule
workflowEn.detail: Mark useful drafts and noise before turning the review into a recurring task.

checkEn: Review recipients, content, attachments, and sensitive data before you send every message yourself.

workflowEn.label: Connect the repository
workflowEn.detail: Install Codex code review and authorize the intended repository or organization.

workflowEn.label: Set review criteria
workflowEn.detail: Add project rules to AGENTS.md, including security, tests, and behavior to preserve.

workflowEn.label: Request a PR review
workflowEn.detail: Enable automatic review or mention @codex review with the areas to emphasize.

workflowEn.label: Assess and fix
workflowEn.detail: Have a developer confirm findings, apply focused fixes, and run regression tests.

checkEn: A person must confirm every finding and test result before approving the merge.

workflowEn.label: Describe the core task
workflowEn.detail: Define the data, primary screen, and key actions users need in the app.

workflowEn.label: Structure the window
workflowEn.detail: Choose the scene model and build a NavigationSplitView with explicit selection state.

workflowEn.label: Add desktop commands
workflowEn.detail: Connect actions to menus, toolbars, keyboard shortcuts, and Settings.

workflowEn.label: Build and test
workflowEn.detail: Run build checks and use an AppKit bridge only where SwiftUI cannot provide the behavior.

checkEn: Test selection, menus, shortcuts, Settings persistence, and accessibility on a real Mac.

workflowEn.label: Provide references
workflowEn.detail: Attach screenshots at key sizes and states, with the expected behavior.

workflowEn.label: Inspect the current system
workflowEn.detail: Find existing components, tokens, routing, and data patterns to reuse.

workflowEn.label: Implement the design
workflowEn.detail: Match hierarchy, spacing, and responsive behavior without duplicating the design system.

workflowEn.label: Compare in the browser
workflowEn.detail: Use Playwright on desktop and mobile, then refine visual and interaction differences.

checkEn: Visually and functionally verify responsiveness, keyboard access, contrast, and core behavior.

workflowEn.label: Prepare the apps
workflowEn.detail: Open and sign in to the required apps, and avoid two chats controlling the same app at once.

workflowEn.label: State the outcome
workflowEn.detail: Start with @Computer and describe the task, apps, and completed result you expect.

workflowEn.label: Monitor the work
workflowEn.detail: Watch picture-in-picture and approve access only when requested.

workflowEn.label: Verify the changes
workflowEn.detail: Ask for an action summary and inspect the final state in each source app.

checkEn: Watch steps involving sensitive data or irreversible actions, then verify results in the source apps.

workflowEn.label: Narrow the scope
workflowEn.detail: Choose a feature, directory, or request flow instead of asking about the whole system.

workflowEn.label: Trace the data path
workflowEn.detail: Follow a request through business logic, persistence, and the UI.

workflowEn.label: Find critical points
workflowEn.detail: Locate validation, side effects, state transitions, and easy-to-miss dependencies.

workflowEn.label: Build a reading list
workflowEn.detail: List the next files, risk areas, and tests to run before making changes.

checkEn: Confirm the explanation against code, tests, and system owners before changing side-effecting behavior.

workflowEn.label: Name the bug sources
workflowEn.detail: Scope the Sentry, Slack, Linear, GitHub, logs, and support queues to review.

workflowEn.label: Run a first scan
workflowEn.detail: Create a P0-P3 list, group duplicates, and separate observed evidence from inference.

workflowEn.label: Improve the queue
workflowEn.detail: Remove known alerts, add missing sources, and define minimum evidence for each bug.

workflowEn.label: Make it recurring
workflowEn.detail: Schedule triage only after it is reliable, and draft handoffs or issues for team review.

checkEn: System owners should confirm severity, duplicate groups, and evidence before issues or alerts are created.

workflowEn.label: Open the target
workflowEn.detail: Open the relevant route or component and prepare the target viewport or reference image.

workflowEn.label: Request one change
workflowEn.detail: Change only one item per round: spacing, alignment, color, copy, or state.

workflowEn.label: Keep the edit small
workflowEn.detail: Reuse existing components and tokens without touching unrelated routing or data flow.

workflowEn.label: Review and iterate
workflowEn.detail: Inspect the browser result, then give the next focused adjustment in the same chat.

checkEn: Check other states, breakpoints, and the component's accessibility for unintended effects.

workflowEn.label: Connect Slack and the repo
workflowEn.detail: Install the Slack app and map the correct channel, repository, and environment.

workflowEn.label: Assign work in a thread
workflowEn.detail: Mention @Codex with the problem, scope, constraints, and expected output.

workflowEn.label: Follow the chat link
workflowEn.detail: Review Codex's analysis, changes, and verification from the linked chat.

workflowEn.label: Review and refine
workflowEn.detail: Check the diff and tests, then request focused follow-up changes in the thread.

checkEn: Confirm the repository and environment, then review the diff and test results before merging.

workflowEn.label: Set data boundaries
workflowEn.detail: Define allowed sources, start dates, usable fields, and sensitive data to exclude.

workflowEn.label: Review the cohort
workflowEn.detail: Count and group hires by team and manager, then flag missing readiness information.

workflowEn.label: Draft the package
workflowEn.detail: Prepare a tracker, team summary, welcome-space plan, and welcome messages for review.

workflowEn.label: Approve each action
workflowEn.detail: Check names, access, privacy, and copy before allowing writes, invites, or posts.

checkEn: Verify access rights, announcement status, and every personal-data field before taking action.

workflowEn.label: Select the meeting
workflowEn.detail: Name the customer, date, or meeting and retrieve its transcript and AI Companion summary.

workflowEn.label: Extract key outcomes
workflowEn.detail: Capture decisions, risks, opportunities, owners, and dates with supporting evidence.

workflowEn.label: Draft each destination
workflowEn.detail: Prepare a customer email, account-plan update, CRM note, and Slack message, separating internal details.

workflowEn.label: Review before sending
workflowEn.detail: Have the account owner review and approve each send, post, or record update separately.

checkEn: Verify commitments, owners, dates, customer data, and internal details before publishing anywhere.

workflowEn.label: Set a learning goal
workflowEn.detail: Name the concept, source material, and specific questions instead of asking for a broad summary.

workflowEn.label: Split the reading
workflowEn.detail: Use subagents for structure, foundations, figures, equations, and evidence quality.

workflowEn.label: Synthesize the report
workflowEn.detail: Create a Markdown brief with a summary, glossary, concept map, evidence table, and caveats.

workflowEn.label: Review and experiment
workflowEn.detail: Ask follow-ups and add a small example or notebook when the concept needs a working demonstration.

checkEn: Ensure the report separates source claims from interpretation and cites the relevant pages, figures, or tables.

workflowEn.label: Define the problem
workflowEn.detail: Name the target user, core problem, and one workflow the prototype must prove.

workflowEn.label: Explore a visual direction
workflowEn.detail: Use ImageGen for mockups and choose the direction that communicates the idea clearly.

workflowEn.label: Build the MVP
workflowEn.detail: Create the smallest working prototype with the right plugin for a website or game.

workflowEn.label: Test in the browser
workflowEn.detail: Use Playwright on the main workflow and iterate from team feedback.

checkEn: Ask target users to try the core flow and separate what the prototype proves from remaining assumptions.

workflowEn.label: Clarify the inputs
workflowEn.detail: Provide the repo, screenshot, design brief, data source, and anything that must not change.

workflowEn.label: Build and check locally
workflowEn.detail: Use Build Web Apps to update the project and run its required build or checks.

workflowEn.label: Deploy a preview
workflowEn.detail: Use the Vercel plugin and report missing environment variables or sign-in steps.

workflowEn.label: Inspect the live URL
workflowEn.detail: Check desktop and mobile, then fix issues using build logs or observed page behavior.

checkEn: Review the preview, environment variables, and secrets, then seek separate approval for production deployment.

workflowEn.label: Prepare the environment
workflowEn.detail: Choose local, staging, or production-like, with the needed account, test data, and flags.

workflowEn.label: Choose the main flows
workflowEn.detail: Prioritize hero flows and decide whether testing stops or continues after a blocker.

workflowEn.label: Run the test
workflowEn.detail: Use @Computer to click, type, and inspect function, layout, and copy within scope.

workflowEn.label: Triage the report
workflowEn.detail: Capture repro steps, expected and actual results, severity, and recommended order of fixes.

checkEn: A person should reproduce critical bugs and confirm the test account cannot affect real data.

workflowEn.label: Inventory the current setup
workflowEn.detail: Map the models, endpoints, prompts, tools, and response shapes used in the repo.

workflowEn.label: Read current guidance
workflowEn.detail: Use $openai-docs for target-model guidance, API features, and prompting practices.

workflowEn.label: Migrate in scope
workflowEn.detail: Change only what is required and flag behavior or schema choices for human review.

workflowEn.label: Measure before release
workflowEn.detail: Compare tests and evals with baseline quality, latency, and cost.

checkEn: Review official docs, response shapes, tool behavior, eval quality, and cost before rollout.

workflowEn.label: Choose the conversation
workflowEn.detail: Name the sender or exact thread ChatGPT should review.

workflowEn.label: Extract the request
workflowEn.detail: Summarize the ask, missing details, and apps needed before acting.

workflowEn.label: Work across apps
workflowEn.detail: Find information, check calendars, or prepare requested items using signed-in sessions.

workflowEn.label: Draft and stop
workflowEn.detail: Draft the reply in the original thread and ask before buying, booking, paying, or confirming.

checkEn: Verify the recipient, final details, price, timing, and cancellation terms before approving any action.

workflowEn.label: Choose the exact frame
workflowEn.detail: Link directly to the source-of-truth frame, component, or variant.

workflowEn.label: Read the design context
workflowEn.detail: Use the Figma skill to inspect variables, assets, screenshots, and variant details.

workflowEn.label: Fit the existing system
workflowEn.detail: Reuse the repo's components, tokens, routing, and data patterns instead of copying reference code.

workflowEn.label: Compare and refine
workflowEn.detail: Use Playwright to check spacing, hierarchy, interaction, and responsive behavior against Figma.

checkEn: Verify variants, asset rights, responsive states, and accessibility against Figma and the design system.

workflowEn.label: Confirm scope and access
workflowEn.detail: Name the authorized repository or folder and do not expand beyond it.

workflowEn.label: Set security context
workflowEn.detail: Put architecture, trust boundaries, invariants, exclusions, and severity rules in SECURITY.md; keep build and test commands in AGENTS.md.

workflowEn.label: Run a deep scan
workflowEn.detail: Let the plugin repeat discovery, validation, and attack-path analysis, then produce finding reports and hardening guidance.

workflowEn.label: Review before fixing
workflowEn.detail: Inspect the scan directory, report.md, detailed findings, hardening portfolio, and proof gaps before fixing one finding.

checkEn: A security expert must confirm reachability, severity, and impact before disclosure or remediation.

workflowEn.label: Write the game plan
workflowEn.detail: Define the core loop, controls, win and fail states, difficulty, and milestones in PLAN.md.

workflowEn.label: Set working rules
workflowEn.detail: Use AGENTS.md to require plan tracking, progress notes, and the right skills.

workflowEn.label: Build gameplay and art
workflowEn.detail: Create the game, UI, and assets with saved image prompts for visual consistency.

workflowEn.label: Playtest repeatedly
workflowEn.detail: Use Playwright in the browser and refine controls, feedback, difficulty, and layout.

checkEn: Have real players check controls, states, accessibility, performance, and asset suitability before release.

workflowEn.label: Set the business question
workflowEn.detail: Start with one specific question and the decision the audience will make.

workflowEn.label: Inspect and clean
workflowEn.detail: Review files, columns, quality, candidate keys, and match rates before joining data.

workflowEn.label: Analyze reproducibly
workflowEn.detail: Use saved scripts and artifacts for transforms and visuals, starting with explainable models.

workflowEn.label: Package the result
workflowEn.detail: Deliver a memo, spreadsheet, report, or dashboard with limits and rerun instructions.

checkEn: Review join keys, missing data, leakage, model assumptions, and business meaning before publishing conclusions.
